name: street-fighter
title: Street Fighter (with video intros)
prompt: Build a Street Fighter game. a blue-gi player versus a red-gi CPU fighter, always facing each other on a dojo stage. Each fighter is a detailed martial artist that needs animated poses seeded from its idle — idle, walk, jump, crouch/duck, block, punch (arm fully extended, fist forward), kick (leg raised high toward the opponent), throw-fireball (arm thrust forward as a projectile launches), hit/stagger, and a dramatic death/KO fall — at least 2 frames from idle per action so each move reads clearly. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right move, ArrowUp jump, ArrowDown duck, J punch, K kick, L fireball, R restart. A health bar per fighter at the top; punches, kicks, and fireballs drain the opponent's health, and reaching zero triggers the death animation and a round-end screen. The CPU approaches, attacks up close, throws fireballs at range, and jumps or ducks to dodge. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for the cinematic beats — a pre-fight intro (both fighters face off in a dojo) and a KO victory clip when a fighter's health hits zero — each seeded from a generated key-art still via image-to-video so the clips match the sprite art; play as muted full-screen overlays any key skips, and the round fight itself stays sprite-animated on the canvas. High-resolution detailed sprites, transparent backgrounds, crisp shading, plus hit sounds and looping fight music. Fighters always face each other. Kick and punch hitboxes must travel toward the opponent — if the player is left of CPU, kick extends with positive horizontal reach toward +x; mirror sprite with scaleX when facing left but do not invert damage direction. Expose facing on state. Movement from crouch must not block arrow keys.

name: donkey-kong
title: Donkey Kong
prompt: Build a Donkey Kong game. Single-screen arcade platformer: A plumber hero climbs a stack of sloped girders connected by ladders to rescue a captured figure at the top, while a big ape rolls barrels down toward him. The hero is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — idle, run (legs cycling), climb (arms reaching up a ladder), jump (legs tucked mid-leap), and a hit/fall — at least 2 frames from idle for each. BARRELS: each barrel MUST stay on the girder surface and ROLL DOWN the slope (follow platform yAt(x) while moving toward the low end). At the low end / ladder gap it TUMBLES down onto the next girder, reverses direction, and keeps rolling — never free-fall off the first floor into empty air or straight to the bottom. Jumping over a barrel scores points; touching one loses a life. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right run, ArrowUp/Down climb ladders, Space jump, R restart. Reach the top to win the level; lose all lives to game over. Colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds. Generated PNGs must be drawn with drawImage (not fillRect placeholders). Barrel hazard loop must keep spawning — do not one-shot spawn.

name: centipede
title: Centipede
prompt: Build a Centipede game. Fixed shooter: The player controls a blaster at the bottom that moves freely in the lower zone and fires upward (blaster art: muzzle points UP; shots leave the front). A centipede has a distinct head, connected body segments, and a tail that follow the head as one chain (history trail — not independent movers) and winds down through a field of mushrooms; shooting a middle segment splits it into two, and shooting the head scores most. When a shot hits a body segment it leaves a mushroom. The blaster needs idle and firing frames; the centipede head and body are sprites. Controls: Arrow keys move, Space fire, R restart. Clearing the whole centipede advances the wave (faster, longer); a centipede segment or spider reaching the player loses a life. Score and lives on a HUD. Crisp colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds.

name: pac-man
title: Pac-Man
prompt: Build a Pac-Man game. Maze game: A yellow chomping hero navigates a walled maze eating dots; four colored ghosts chase him with simple distinct behaviors. The hero needs an animated chomp (mouth opening and closing, facing the move direction); ghosts are sprites with eyes that look toward their movement. Four power-pellets in the corners turn the ghosts frightened (blue) for a few seconds so the hero can eat them for bonus points. Controls: Arrow keys to steer (queued turns), R restart. Eat every dot to clear the level; a ghost catching the hero when not powered loses a life. HUD shows score and lives. Colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, dark maze walls. MECHANICS (any theme): COPY a compact connected maze template — do NOT hand-author rows from scratch. Use tile coords (tx,ty) for player AND chasers (state.chasers[] or state.enemies[]); derive pixels at draw. Scan maze for corridor spawn cells. Chasers must move every frame with the same wall checks as the player. Power pickup sets state.vulnerableTimer (or per-chaser flee mode) for a few seconds. Expose collectiblesLeft or dotsRemaining on window.state.

name: space-invaders
title: Space Invaders
prompt: Build a Space Invaders game. Fixed shooter: A player cannon at the bottom moves left/right and fires upward at a grid of alien invaders that march side to side, dropping down and speeding up as their numbers thin. Aliens have a simple 2-frame animation (legs alternating). A few destructible bunkers shield the player, and occasionally a UFO crosses the top for bonus points. Aliens drop bombs. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right move, Space fire, R restart. Clear all aliens to advance the wave; an alien bomb hitting the player, or the aliens reaching the bottom, loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Classic arcade alien silhouettes (crab, squid, octopus rows) — NOT generic bugs. Crisp retro high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds.

name: asteroids
title: Asteroids
prompt: Build an Asteroids game. Vector shooter: A triangular ship sits in open space, rotates left/right, thrusts in the direction it faces (momentum carries it with gentle drag), and fires bullets forward. Large asteroids drift and, when shot, split into medium then small fragments before being destroyed — the asteroids must be IRREGULAR jagged polygons, NOT perfect circles. The screen wraps at all edges. Occasionally a flying saucer appears and shoots back. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right rotate, ArrowUp thrust, Space fire, R restart. Clearing all asteroids spawns a denser wave; a collision with an asteroid or saucer bullet loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Clean glowing vector line art on black. Irregular jagged asteroid polygons; vx=Math.cos(angle)*speed, vy=Math.sin(angle)*speed. If TEST OK on first build with probes green, emit <done/> — do not keep patching a working game.

name: frogger
title: Frogger
prompt: Build a Frogger game. Crossing game: A frog starts at the bottom and must cross a busy multi-lane road (cars and trucks moving at different speeds and directions) then a river (hopping on floating logs and turtles; water is deadly) to reach home slots at the top. The frog hops one grid cell per key press and needs idle and mid-hop (legs extended) frames facing its move direction. Controls: Arrow keys hop, R restart. Filling all home slots wins the level; getting hit by traffic, falling in the water, or running out of time loses a life. HUD with score, lives, and a timer. Colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds. Generated PNGs → drawImage. Hop one cell per keydown edge (not key-hold spam).

name: galaga
title: Galaga
prompt: Build a Galaga game. Formation shooter: The player ship at the bottom moves left/right and fires upward. Insect-like enemies fly in along curving paths, assemble into a formation at the top, then peel off in diving attack runs while firing at the player. Enemies have a 2-frame wing-flap animation. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right move, Space fire, R restart. Clear the wave to advance (more enemies, more aggressive dives); being hit by an enemy or its shot loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Crisp colorful high-resolution sprites on a starfield, transparent backgrounds.

name: tetris
title: Tetris
prompt: Build a Tetris game. Falling-block puzzle: Seven tetromino shapes fall one at a time into a vertical well; the player moves them left/right, rotates them, and soft/hard-drops them. Completed horizontal lines clear and award points (more for multiple lines at once), and the fall speed increases with level. Show the next piece and a ghost preview of where the current piece will land. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right move, ArrowUp rotate, ArrowDown soft drop, Space hard drop, R restart. The game ends when the stack reaches the top. HUD with score, level, and lines cleared. Clean colorful blocks with crisp borders on a dark well.

name: breakout
title: Breakout
prompt: Build a Breakout game. Brick-breaker: A paddle at the bottom moves left/right and bounces a ball up into a wall of colored bricks; clearing bricks scores points, and the ball's angle depends on where it strikes the paddle. Some bricks take two hits; occasional power-ups fall (wider paddle, multi-ball) and are caught with the paddle. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right or mouse move the paddle, Space launch ball, R restart. Clear all bricks to advance to a new layout; losing the ball off the bottom loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Crisp colorful high-resolution bricks, a glossy ball, dark background.

name: snake
title: Snake
prompt: Build a Snake game. A snake moves continuously on a grid, growing one segment each time it eats food that appears at a random cell; speed increases as it grows. The snake turns with the arrow keys but cannot reverse directly into itself. Controls: Arrow keys steer, R restart. The game ends when the snake hits a wall or its own body. Include a wraparound-walls toggle. HUD with current score (length) and high score. Clean high-resolution rounded snake segments and bright food on a dark grid.

name: dig-dug
title: Dig Dug
prompt: Build a Dig Dug game. A digger moves through solid earth, carving tunnels in any of four directions, and fights underground monsters by pumping them up with an air harpoon until they pop, or by dropping rocks on them. The digger needs idle, dig (per direction), and pump (arm extending the harpoon) frames; monsters have a walk and an inflating animation. Controls: Arrow keys move/dig, Space pump, R restart. Clear all monsters to advance the level; a monster touching the digger loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Colorful high-resolution sprites, layered soil colors, transparent backgrounds.

name: qbert
title: Q*bert
prompt: Build a Q*bert game. Isometric hopper: A character hops diagonally across a pyramid of cubes; each cube changes color when landed on, and the goal is to recolor every cube. The hero needs idle and mid-hop frames for each diagonal direction. Enemies bounce down the pyramid and must be avoided; hopping off the edge falls to its death. Controls: the four arrow keys map to the four diagonal hops (up-left, up-right, down-left, down-right), R restart. Recolor all cubes to clear the level; touching an enemy or falling off loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Crisp colorful isometric cubes, high-resolution character sprites, transparent backgrounds.

name: missile-command
title: Missile Command
prompt: Build a Missile Command game. Defense game: Enemy missiles streak down from the top toward six cities along the bottom; the player aims a crosshair and fires counter-missiles from a base that explode into expanding blasts, destroying any incoming missiles caught in the radius. Controls: mouse moves the crosshair and click fires, or Arrow keys move the crosshair and Space fires; R restart. Each wave sends more and faster missiles; the round ends when all cities are destroyed. Score per missile intercepted, bonus for surviving cities. HUD with score and remaining cities. Glowing high-resolution explosions and missile trails on a night-sky background. If TEST OK on first build with probes green, emit <done/> — do not keep patching a working game.

name: joust
title: Joust
prompt: Build a Joust game. Flap-and-joust game: The player rides a flying ostrich and gains altitude by repeatedly flapping (each press gives an upward impulse against gravity); the goal is to collide with enemy knights on buzzards from ABOVE to defeat them, while avoiding being hit from above yourself. The mount needs a flap animation (wings up/down) and a walk frame. Defeated enemies drop an egg to collect before it hatches. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right move, Space flap, R restart. Clear all enemies to advance the wave; being struck from above or touching the lava loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Colorful high-resolution sprites, platforms over lava, transparent backgrounds. Generated PNGs → drawImage; platforms over lava; flap gives upward impulse against gravity.

name: bomberman
title: Bomberman
prompt: Build a Bomberman game. Grid game: A character moves on a tile grid and drops bombs that explode in a plus-shaped blast after a short fuse, destroying soft blocks (which sometimes reveal power-ups: more bombs, longer blast range) and any enemy or player caught in the flames. The hero needs idle and walk frames per direction. Simple enemies wander the maze. Controls: Arrow keys move, Space drop bomb, R restart. Clear all enemies and reach the exit to advance; being caught in any blast or touched by an enemy loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Colorful high-resolution top-down sprites, transparent backgrounds.

name: pong
title: Pong
prompt: Build a Pong game. Two paddles (player on the left, CPU on the right) move vertically and bounce a ball between them; the ball speeds up over a rally and its bounce angle depends on where it strikes the paddle. The CPU paddle tracks the ball with a slight lag so it stays beatable. Controls: ArrowUp/Down or W/S move the player paddle, R restart. First to 11 points wins; a point is scored when the ball passes the opponent's paddle. A dashed center net and each side's score at the top. Clean crisp white paddles and ball on a black court, high-resolution.

name: super-mario
title: Super Mario Platformer (with video cutscenes)
prompt: Build a Super Mario game. Side-scrolling platformer: A hero runs and jumps through a scrolling level with floating brick and question blocks, pipes, pits, and patrolling enemies you defeat by stomping from above. The hero is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — idle, run (legs cycling), jump (arm up, legs tucked), and a hit/shrink — at least 2 frames from idle for each. Coins to collect and a flag/goal at the end. The camera scrolls to follow the hero. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right run, Space or ArrowUp jump, R restart. Reach the goal to win; falling in a pit or touching an enemy from the side loses a life. HUD with coins, score, and lives. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for an intro before the level starts and a goal-pole victory celebration when the hero reaches the flag (each seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable full-screen overlays, platforming stays on the scrolling canvas. Colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, plus jump/coin SFX and looping level music.

name: kung-fu-master
title: Kung-Fu Master (with video cutscenes)
prompt: Build a Kung-Fu Master game. Side-scrolling beat-'em-up: A martial-artist hero walks left/right through a scrolling stage and fights waves of enemies who close in from both sides. The hero is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — idle, walk, crouch, punch (arm fully extended), kick (leg raised high), and a hit/knockdown — at least 2 frames from idle for each so the strikes read clearly. Enemies (grippers and knife-throwers) approach and attack; defeating them all advances toward a boss at the end of the floor. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right walk, ArrowDown crouch, J punch, K kick, R restart. A health bar for the hero; losing all health is game over. HUD with score, health, and a level timer. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for a floor-intro establishing shot and a dramatic boss-entrance clip before the end-of-floor fight (seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable overlays, the beat-'em-up combat stays sprite-animated. High-resolution detailed sprites, transparent backgrounds, plus punch/kick SFX and tense looping music.

title: 1942 (vertical shooter)
prompt: Build a 1942 game. Vertical-scrolling shoot-'em-up: The player flies a fighter plane upward over a continuously scrolling sea; enemy planes fly in formation patterns from the top and fire back, and the player shoots upward and can perform a brief barrel-roll to dodge bullets. The player plane has a banking animation when moving left/right; enemy planes are sprites with a propeller shimmer. Power-ups upgrade the player's gun. Controls: Arrow keys move, Space fire, Shift roll/dodge, R restart. Survive the wave to advance (denser enemies, an occasional boss bomber); being hit by an enemy or a bullet loses a life. HUD with score and lives. Crisp top-down high-resolution sprites over a scrolling ocean, transparent backgrounds.

name: doom
title: Doom / Wolfenstein 3D (FPS + video)
prompt: Build a Doom game. First-person 3D maze shooter using three.js from CDN (Scene + PerspectiveCamera + WebGLRenderer — do NOT hand-roll a canvas DDA raycaster): The player walks through a textured maze of walls, floor, and ceiling with mouse-look (pointer-lock) and WASD movement, shooting monsters that approach as billboard sprites always facing the camera. Each monster needs an idle frame and an attack/hit frame; the first-person weapon is a screen-space overlay with idle and muzzle-flash firing frames (the muzzle points UP in the source art). A crosshair sits at screen center and the camera does all the aiming. Controls: WASD move, mouse look, click or Space fire, R restart. Pick up health and ammo; clear all monsters to finish the level, dying at zero health. HUD with health, ammo, score, and a small top-right overview map. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for a level-intro establishing shot (corridor or hellscape) and a boss-reveal clip before the final encounter (seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable full-screen overlays, FPS gameplay stays in the three.js scene. High-resolution detailed wall textures and monster sprites, transparent backgrounds on the sprites, plus gunfire/explosion SFX and ominous looping music.

name: minecraft
title: Minecraft (voxel sandbox)
prompt: Build a Minecraft game. First-person voxel sandbox with three.js: The player explores a 3D world of cube blocks (grass, dirt, stone) on chunked terrain, with mouse-look (pointer-lock) and WASD movement plus space-to-jump under gravity. Left-click breaks the targeted block and right-click places the selected block; raycast from the camera to highlight the block face under the crosshair. A small hotbar selects the block type. Controls: WASD move, Space jump, mouse look, left/right click break/place, number keys select block, R restart. High-resolution blocky voxel textures, a bright sky, and simple ambient lighting. Expose the world state for verification.

name: outrun
title: OutRun / Pole Position (racing + video)
prompt: Build an OutRun game. Pseudo-3D Mode-7-like racing road: The player drives a car at the bottom of the screen, steering left/right across lanes, accelerating and braking, while rival cars and roadside scenery (trees, signs) scale up as they approach. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right steer, ArrowUp accelerate, ArrowDown brake, R restart. A countdown timer extends at checkpoints; the race ends when time runs out or you reach the finish, and crashing into traffic or scenery slows you down. HUD with speed, time, and distance. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for the cinematic beats Out Run is famous for — a sun-drenched coastal intro before the race, a checkpoint celebration, and a finish-line victory clip (each seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable overlays, the race itself stays on the Mode-7 road. High-resolution detailed car sprite and colorful roadside scenery, transparent backgrounds, a perspective road with painted lane stripes, plus engine SFX and driving music.

name: chess
title: Chess (holochess — animation-first, player vs CPU)
prompt: Build a holochess game. 8x8 board (or circular holochess table): THE #1 DELIVERABLE IS EXCELLENT ANIMATION — rules and CPU are required, but a chess game that teleports pieces or holds a single frozen sprite is a failure even if the logic is correct. Every legal move must PLAY OUT as a visible performance: monsters walk, hop, or glide square-by-square along the path (never snap board[] and redraw — tween drawX/drawY and commit board[] only when the animation finishes). While moving, cycle walk frames on a timer (~120ms per frame); knights get a visible hop arc; castling slides king and rook together; pawn promotion plays a brief transform; check makes the king monster shake or glow. Idle pieces get a subtle breathing loop. On capture, play the signature two-beat BEFORE the victim disappears: (1) lift — capturer raises victim overhead, (2) slam — drives victim down; generate dedicated lift and slam poses per piece type. Block all clicks while state.animating; expose state.animating, state.capturePhase, and moving piece draw coords for probes. ART: every piece is a distinct fantasy MONSTER (not Unicode glyphs) with silhouettes that read as pawn/knight/bishop/rook/queen/king — small grunts, leaping beast-riders, tall mystics, fortress-beasts, multi-limbed queens, crowned leaders; light vs dark palettes. Generate high-resolution transparent sprites for BOTH sides: idle + at least THREE walk/hop frames per type, plus lift and slam poses for capturers. Use the SAME character description in every asset prompt (fixed seed) so walk/lift/slam match idle. Flip sprites to face direction of travel. PLAYER VS COMPUTER: human White/Light bottom moves first; computer Black/Dark top replies automatically after each human move — not hotseat, no AI-off default. Block human input during CPU think AND during any piece animation. CPU: negamax + alpha-beta depth 3, material eval (P=1 N/B=3 R=5 Q=9), scheduled with setTimeout(200-400ms). RULES: click select + legal-target commit; full chess including capture, check, checkmate. Controls: mouse click, R restart. HUD: turn, captures tray, check/checkmate, 'CPU thinking…', legal-move highlights. Generated PNGs → drawImage. Tween drawX/drawY; block input while state.animating; lift/slam before capture remove. CPU uses setTimeout(200-400ms).

name: zelda
title: Zelda (overworld action-RPG + video)
prompt: Build a Zelda game. Top-down action-RPG: A hero explores a tile-based overworld of grass, water, trees, and dungeons, talks to NPCs (dialog boxes), collects items (keys, hearts), and fights enemies with a sword. The hero is an animated character needing poses from idle — idle and walk (legs cycling) for each of the four facing directions, plus a sword-attack (arm and blade extended) and a hit frame — at least 2 frames from idle for each. Enemies patrol and chase; hearts track health. Controls: Arrow keys move, Z or Space sword attack, X use item, Enter talk/confirm, R restart. The camera follows the hero room to room. HUD with hearts, keys, and rupees. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for the opening legend (triforce backstory before gameplay) and a victory clip when the dungeon boss is defeated (seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable overlays, overworld exploration and sword fights stay on the tile map. Colorful high-resolution top-down sprites, transparent backgrounds, a tiled overworld, plus sword/enemy SFX and adventure music.

name: monkey-island
title: Monkey Island (point-and-click + video)
prompt: Build a Monkey Island game. Point-and-click adventure: The screen shows a hand-illustrated scene with clickable hotspots; clicking the ground walks the character there (the character needs idle and a walk cycle facing the move direction), and clicking a hotspot triggers a verb action (look, take, use) with a dialog/description box. Items picked up go into an inventory bar at the bottom and can be used on hotspots to solve simple puzzles and progress to the next scene. Controls: mouse click to walk and interact, click inventory items to select, R restart. A verb and inventory UI along the bottom. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for the story beats — an intro establishing the tropical island and pirate port, plus a short clip when transitioning into each major new location (seeded from a painted key-art still of that scene via image-to-video so the mood matches the hand-drawn backgrounds); muted skippable full-screen overlays, point-and-click gameplay stays on the canvas. High-resolution detailed background scenes, a character sprite with a transparent background, plus comedic SFX and Caribbean-flavored music.

name: dragons-lair
title: Dragon's Lair (visual QTE — flexible scenes)
prompt:
Build a Dragon's Lair game. Laserdisc QTE: scripted peril scenes, timed key reactions, hazards drawn ON TOP of full-screen backgrounds.

USER GOAL WINS — read the goal first:
• Scene count: if the user asks for 10 scenes (or 5, or 12), plan and ship that many — do NOT cap at 8.
• Look and characters: if the user asks for cartoon style, pixel art, different heroes, princess-led, etc., use THAT in every <assets>/<videos> prompt — the default cel-animation knight below is only a fallback when the goal is silent.
• Perils and setting: swap, add, or reorder rooms to match the goal; keep the same QTE mechanics.

VISUAL RULE (always — every scene, any count, any art style):
Three layers each frame: (1) static bg_* stage, (2) hazard sprite that MOVES toward the body part about to get hit, (3) hero sprite on top. The player reads the PICTURE to know when to act; the centered word ('JUMP!', 'DUCK!') only says WHICH key. Duck = threat drops toward the head; jump = gap/ledge moves to the feet; dodge = threat sweeps from that side; sword = lunge at torso.

DEFAULT 8-SCENE TEMPLATE (1983 arcade reference — use when the goal does not specify scenes; otherwise adapt, extend, or replace):
1 Drawbridge (bg_drawbridge) — JUMP: planks crack, gap at feet.
2 Slime hall (bg_tentacle_hall) — SWORD: tentacle lunges from pit.
3 Blade corridor (bg_blade_corridor) — DUCK then JUMP: blade at head, then low blade at legs.
4 Crumbling stairs (bg_crumbling_stairs) — RIGHT then JUMP: rubble from left, abyss below.
5 Bat cavern (bg_bat_cavern) — SWORD then DUCK: swarm, then dive at head.
6 Lava ledge (bg_lava_ledge) — LEFT then JUMP: fireball from right, ledge cracks.
7 Throne antechamber (bg_throne_hall) — RIGHT then SWORD: arch falls, phantom flame.
8 Dragon's lair (bg_dragons_lair) — LEFT then SWORD: dragon fire, jaws snap.
(Add scenes 9–10+ with the same telegraph pattern when the goal asks for more.)

PHASE A: same reply must include <plan>, <criteria>, <probes>, <assets>, <sounds>, <videos> so the pipeline runs up front. Criteria must match YOUR scene list (not necessarily 8).

MEDIA: ONE canonical hero description from the goal's style — reuse VERBATIM on every hero sprite and key-art prompt (change ONLY pose words). bg_* are character-free stages. Count bg_* and hero_* / knight_* pose sprites to match your scene list. Three muted I2V videos (intro, fail, victory) from key_* stills unless the goal says otherwise.

CODE: SCENES array sized to your plan; drawImage(bg) → animate hazard → hero pose; performance.now() windows; 0-index state.scene; window.state + window.game.reset; on keydown set lastInputCode/inputFlash; onended/onerror always continues; requestAnimationFrame(frame) immediately; tolerate missing media. Single HTML file.

name: battlezone
title: Battlezone (1980 vector tank)
prompt: Build a Battlezone game. 2D wireframe vector tank game — NOT three.js, NOT sprites, NOT <assets>: First-person wireframe tank combat: wireframe mountains and a ground grid recede toward a horizon; the player sees their tank body and turret as vector outlines in the lower view; enemy tanks and an occasional flying saucer appear as wireframe shapes on the battlefield and shoot back. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right rotate the turret (or hull), ArrowUp/Down drive forward/back, Space fire a vector projectile, R restart. Destroy enemies for score; a hit on the player costs a life. Include a classic radar/minimap in the corner showing enemy blips. HUD shows score and lives. Clean glowing vector line art on black; expose window.state with player yaw/position and enemy list for probes. ArrowLeft = turn/turret left (decrease yaw); ArrowRight = turn right (increase yaw). Do not swap or invert — reversed controls fail immediately. ArrowUp/Down = drive forward/back.

name: star-wars
title: Star Wars (1983 vector trench)
prompt: Build a Star Wars game. 2D wireframe vector trench run — NOT three.js, NOT sprites, NOT <assets>: First-person wireframe flight down a Death Star trench: converging trench walls, tower blocks, and TIE fighter wireframes approach from ahead; a fixed crosshair sits at screen center and the player fires glowing vector bolts. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right (or mouse) steer laterally in the trench, Space fire, R restart. Score points for destroying towers and fighters; reach the exhaust port at the end to win; collision with trench walls or an obstacle loses a life. HUD shows score and lives. Clean glowing vector line art on black; expose window.state with player lateral position, obstacles[], and projectiles for probes. Crosshair moves on both axes: ArrowLeft/Right = lateral; ArrowUp/ArrowDown = vertical aim (crosshair Y or trench pitch). Space fires at crosshair position — not lateral-only steering.

name: particle-fireworks
title: Particle Fireworks Demo
prompt: Build a Particle Fireworks game. Click or press Space to launch a burst of colorful particles from the bottom center; each burst spawns 80-150 particles with random velocity, gravity, and fade-out life. Particles use additive blending for a glowing look. Include auto-launcher every few seconds. HUD shows burst count. Controls: click or Space launch, R restart. Cap total particles to keep FPS stable. Expose window.state with particles[] for probes.

name: simcity-lite
title: SimCity Lite (city builder)
prompt: Build a SimCity game. Click to place residential, commercial, and industrial zones plus roads; each building type costs money and generates income every tick. Show money and population in the HUD. A tool palette selects the active zone type (number keys 1-3). Controls: click place, 1/2/3 select zone, R restart. Tiles render as colored squares with simple icons. Expose window.state with tiles[][], resources, and buildings for probes.

name: elite-trader
title: Elite Trader (space trading)
prompt: Build an Elite Trader game. A starmap shows 8-10 star systems connected by lines; click a system within fuel range to jump (consumes fuel). Each system has a market with buy/sell prices for ore, food, and luxuries — prices vary per system. Cargo hold limits purchases; profit by buying low and selling high. Include pirate encounters on some jumps. HUD: credits, fuel, cargo. Controls: click system to travel, buy/sell buttons in market view, R restart. Expose window.state with systems[], ship fuel/cargo/money.

name: fighter-showcase
title: Fighter Animation Showcase
prompt: Build a Fighter Showcase game. Single-character animation showcase on one stage — NO opponent, just ONE fighter centered on screen. Generate high-resolution transparent sprite art for idle, walk, jump, crouch, punch (arm fully extended), kick (leg raised), and block — at least 2 frames from idle per action. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right walk, ArrowUp jump, ArrowDown crouch, J punch, K kick, L block, R restart. HUD labels the current move name. Expose window.state.move for probes.

name: solitaire
title: Klondike Solitaire
prompt: Build a Solitaire game. Standard layout: stock, waste, four foundations (Ace up by suit), and seven tableau piles with face-down cards underneath. Click/drag to move legal stacks; auto-flip revealed cards. Double-click sends Aces to foundations when legal. Win when all foundations are complete. Render each card with rank and suit visible (not blank rectangles). Controls: mouse drag-and-drop, R reshuffle deal. Expose window.state with piles or tableau for probes.

name: angry-blocks
title: Angry Blocks (slingshot physics)
prompt: Build an Angry Blocks game. A launcher at the left aims a projectile with drag or angle keys; show a faint trajectory preview while aiming. Launch fires a ball under gravity at stacked block targets; knocking blocks scores points. Limited shots per level; clear all blocks to win. Controls: drag or ArrowUp/Down adjust angle, Space launch, R restart. Expose window.state.projectile and state.targets for probes.

name: torch-dungeon
title: Torch Dungeon (lit maze)
prompt: Build a Torch Dungeon game. The visibility radius follows the player; everything outside is shadow/fog-of-darkness — NOT a fully visible grid. Navigate corridors with arrow keys; patrolling guards appear only when lit. Reach the exit to win; caught by a guard loses. Controls: Arrow keys move, R restart. Expose window.state with player position and guards for probes.

name: mobile-snake
title: Mobile Snake (touch controls)
prompt: Build a Mobile Snake game. A virtual D-pad in the bottom-left steers the snake; the playfield fills the upper viewport. Snake grows when eating food; game over on wall or self collision. Large touch targets sized for thumbs. Controls: on-screen D-pad, R restart. HUD shows score. Expose window.state for probes.

name: tower-defense
title: Tower Defense (fixed path)
prompt: Build a Tower Defense game. Enemies follow a visible waypoint path from spawn to exit; place turrets on free grass tiles near the path (click to select tower type, click tile to place). Turrets auto-fire at enemies in range; kills earn money for more towers. Leaked enemies cost lives; clear waves to advance with faster/stronger creeps. HUD: money, lives, wave number. Controls: click place towers, R restart. FUN GRAPHICS: generate high-resolution transparent sprite art for each turret type, each creep/enemy type, and each projectile, and draw them with drawImage instead of bare rectangles or circles; keep dynamic text (money, lives, wave) drawn in code with fillText so it updates, and show a visible muzzle flash or projectile when a tower fires. Expose window.state with enemies[], towers[], path for probes.

name: rhythm-tap
title: Rhythm Tap (4-lane)
prompt: Build a Rhythm Tap game. Notes scroll downward toward a fixed hit line; press D/F/J/K (or Arrow keys mapped to lanes) when a note crosses the line. Judge perfect/good/miss by timing window; combo builds score, misses break combo. Simple chart of notes timed to a metronome BPM (no external audio required — visual timing is enough). HUD: score, combo, judgment text flash. Controls: lane keys, R restart. Expose window.state.notes and songTime for probes.

name: cookie-clicker
title: Cookie Clicker (idle)
prompt: Build a Cookie Clicker game. A big cookie button earns currency per click; buy generators (cursor, grandma, farm) that produce passive income per second. Costs scale exponentially; show formatted big numbers. Buttons grey out when unaffordable. HUD: currency counter, CPS, owned generators list. Controls: click cookie, click buy buttons, R restart. Expose window.state.currency and generators for probes.

name: bullet-hell-boss
title: Bullet Hell Boss
prompt: Build a Bullet Hell Boss game. The player ship dodges dense patterned bullet streams (spiral, ring, aimed shots) in an arena; player hitbox is a tiny core much smaller than the sprite. Boss phases switch patterns at HP thresholds. Player can shoot back to damage the boss. HUD: score, lives, boss HP bar. Controls: Arrow keys move (Shift focus/slow), Space fire, R restart. Expose window.state with bullets[], boss hp/phase for probes.

name: typing-race
title: Typing Race
prompt: Build a Typing Race game. Typed letters highlight on the target word; completing a word scores points and spawns the next. Wrong keys flash red briefly. HUD: score, WPM estimate, current word. Controls: keyboard letters (use e.key), R restart. Expose window.state.target and state.typed for probes.

name: pinball
title: Pinball Table
prompt: Build a Pinball game. A ball falls under gravity and bounces off walls, bumpers, and ramps; two flippers at the bottom (Z and / or Left/Right Shift) bat the ball upward. Bumpers score points and add impulse. Drain between flippers loses the ball; plunger relaunch. HUD: score, balls left. Controls: flipper keys, R restart. Expose window.state.ball and flippers for probes.

name: stealth-infiltration
title: Stealth Infiltration
prompt: Build a Stealth Infiltration game. The player sneaks through a room with patrolling guards; each guard has a visible vision cone. Entering a cone fills an alert meter; full alert triggers chase. Reach the green goal zone unseen to win; contact with a guard or full alert loses. Walls block line-of-sight. HUD: alert meter, objective marker. Controls: Arrow keys move, R restart. Expose window.state with guards[], alert for probes.

name: roguelike-dungeon
title: Roguelike Dungeon
prompt: Build a Roguelike Dungeon game. Procedurally generate rooms connected by corridors; fog-of-war reveals tiles near the player. Arrow keys move one tile per turn; monsters move after each player step. Bump attack monsters; pick up loot; descend stairs to deeper harder floors. HUD: HP, depth, gold. Controls: Arrow keys move/attack, R restart new dungeon. Expose window.state.map and player for probes. Colorful high-resolution sprites for the hero, monsters, wall/floor tiles, loot, and stairs, transparent backgrounds — drawn tiles via drawImage, NOT ASCII glyphs or Unicode characters.

name: stacking-tower
title: Stacking Tower
prompt: Build a Stacking Tower game. A crane swings a block left-right above the stack; press Space to drop it. Well-aligned blocks stack and raise the tower/score; misaligned blocks topple or fall off and cost a life. Swing speed increases with height. HUD: height/score, lives. Controls: Space drop, R restart. Expose window.state.stack[] for probes.

name: match-three
title: Match Three (Bejeweled)
prompt: Build a Match Three game. Click or drag to swap two adjacent gems; if the swap creates 3+ in a row or column they clear, gems above fall down, new gems refill from the top, and cascades chain for combo score. Invalid swaps revert. HUD: score, moves remaining. Controls: click/drag swap, R restart. Expose window.state.board for probes.

name: checkers
title: Checkers (candy carnival — player vs CPU)
prompt: Build a Checkers game. 8x8 board — PLAYER VS COMPUTER, not hotseat: The human plays the bright Gold/Candy side from the bottom and moves first; the computer plays the cool Purple/Jelly side from the top and replies automatically after every human move. Block human clicks while the CPU is thinking and show a 'CPU thinking…' HUD line. FUN GRAPHICS: treat every piece like a carnival candy mascot, not a flat disk — glossy gumdrop men for regular pieces (round, shiny, with cute faces) and crowned lollipop kings when promoted; the board is a pastel checkerboard with soft gradients and sparkle highlights on legal-move squares. Generate high-resolution transparent sprite art for gold gumdrop idle + hop frames and purple jelly idle + hop frames, plus a king crown overlay frame. pieces BOUNCE-hop to their destination (2-3 hop frames) instead of teleporting; on a jump capture, play a quick squash-and-stretch landing on the captured square before the victim pops away in a tiny confetti puff. Enforce real checkers rules: diagonal moves on dark squares only, mandatory jumps when available, multi-jump chains in one turn, and king promotion at the far rank with a brief sparkle animation. CPU opponent: negamax + alpha-beta depth 3 with a simple eval (man=1, king=3, small bonus for back-rank kings and center control) — NOT random moves or 1-ply greed. Schedule CPU moves with setTimeout(200-400ms). Controls: mouse click to select and move, R restart. HUD: score/captures tray, whose turn, promotion and king-crown announcements. Expose window.state for probes. Generated PNGs → drawImage; pointerdown/up for moves; CPU negamax setTimeout(200-400ms); block human input while CPU thinking.

name: tower-defense-openfield
title: Tower Defense (open field / Fieldrunners-style)
prompt: Build a Fieldrunners game. Open-field tower defense: No pre-drawn path: grass field with one entrance and one exit; towers on free tiles BUILD THE MAZE — enemies BFS-pathfind around towers (reject any placement that walls the exit; re-path in-flight creeps when the maze changes). Fieldrunners tower set: Rapid Gun, Missile (splash), Goo (slow), Mortar (long range), Tesla (lightning beam), Flame (short-range splash beam). Enemies escalate by wave: soldiers, bikes, jeeps, tanks, helicopters, blimps. Sidebar: click tower type, click grid to place, click placed tower to upgrade or sell. Kills earn money; leaks cost lives; win after clearing a long campaign (~45 waves). HUD: money, lives, wave. Controls: click, Space start wave, R restart. Generate <assets> sprites for each tower, enemy, and projectile; draw with drawImage. ROTATING TURRETS (gun/missile/goo/mortar): fixed base plus 5 pre-drawn head angles (E/SE/S/NE/N; mirror for west); lerp aim toward target, recoil, muzzle flash or projectile — do NOT ctx.rotate one whole tower sprite. BEAM TOWERS (Tesla/Flame): vertical upright sprite; tower stays fixed; on fire draw a large procedural lightning or flame beam from the tower top to the enemy. Expose window.state with enemies[], towers[], grid, path.

name: prince-of-persia
title: Prince of Persia (rotoscoped platformer, with video cutscenes)
prompt: Build a Prince of Persia game. Cinematic side-view platformer in a dungeon: a lone hero runs, does a measured running-jump across gaps, hangs from and climbs up ledges, and crosses hazards (spikes, a slicing blade, a loose floor tile that drops). The hero is a detailed rotoscoped character needing fluid animated poses from idle — idle, walk/run cycle (legs cycling), running-jump (reach + tuck), ledge-hang and pull-up, and a sword stance/strike — at least 2 frames from idle for each so motion reads as weighty and realistic. A guard blocks the path: draw sword (Shift), then attack/parry to defeat it. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right walk/run, ArrowUp climb/pull-up, Space jump, Shift draw sword, R restart. Reach the exit door to win; falling into a pit, spikes, or losing the swordfight loses a life. HUD with a countdown timer and lives. Generate VIDEO CUTSCENES for a title/intro before the level and a victory scene at the exit door (each seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video); muted skippable full-screen overlays, platforming stays on the canvas. High-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, plus footstep/sword SFX and tense looping music.

name: metal-slug
title: Metal Slug (run-and-gun side-scroller)
prompt: Build a Metal Slug run-and-gun game. Side-scrolling action platformer: a commando runs and jumps across a scrolling battlefield of platforms and sandbags while firing a rapid gun at waves of soldiers and a heavy tank/turret. Aim while shooting — fire straight ahead by facing, straight up with ArrowUp, or diagonally when a direction and up are held; you can shoot while standing, running, or jumping. The commando is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — idle, run cycle, jump, and a shoot/recoil pose — at least 2 frames from idle for each; enemies have a hit/explode animation. Grab a weapon pickup for a spread/heavy shot. Controls: ArrowLeft/Right run, Space jump, X or Z fire, ArrowUp aim up, R restart. Clear the enemies and reach the end to win; taking a hit loses a life. HUD with score, ammo, and lives. Crisp colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, punchy gunfire/explosion SFX and driving music.

name: mortal-kombat
title: Mortal Kombat (versus fighter, with fatality video)
prompt: Build a Mortal Kombat fighting game. Two fighters face each other on a single stage, one on the left and one on the right, each with a health bar at the top. Each fighter is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — idle stance (breathing bob), walk toward/away, high punch, low kick, block, a projectile special (e.g. thrown fireball), and a hit/stagger — at least 2 frames from idle for each; fighters always turn to FACE each other. Controls (P1): ArrowLeft/Right move, ArrowUp jump, ArrowDown crouch/block, A punch, S kick, D special; the CPU opponent reacts and attacks. Drain the opponent's health to win the round; best of the bout. When a fighter is finished, trigger a FATALITY: generate a VIDEO CUTSCENE finisher/victory (seeded from key-art stills via image-to-video) as a muted skippable full-screen overlay, then return to the reset() path for a rematch. HUD with two health bars and a round timer. High-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, impactful hit SFX and stage music.

name: rampage
title: Rampage (giant-monster building climb-and-smash)
prompt: Build a Rampage game. A giant monster clings to the face of a tall city skyscraper and climbs it, punching windows and floors to smash the building to rubble while dodging helicopters and gunfire. The monster is a detailed character needing animated poses from idle — cling/idle, climb cycle (limbs reaching up the wall), a punch/smash swing, eat-from-window, and a hit/recoil — at least 2 frames from idle for each; smashed floors visibly crumble and the building shrinks. Grab civilians/food from windows to recover health. Controls: ArrowUp/Down climb, ArrowLeft/Right move across the facade, Space punch/smash, R restart. Demolish the building to win the level (a taller one appears); losing all health from helicopters/gunfire ends the rampage. HUD with score and a health/damage meter. Bold colorful high-resolution sprites, transparent backgrounds, crunchy smash/explosion SFX and a rampaging soundtrack.

name: mr-do
title: Mr. Do!
prompt: Build a Mr. Do! game. A clown digs bright green dirt into black tunnels (four directions), collecting cherries in groups of eight while red dinosaur creeps chase him. Large yellow apples can be PUSHED horizontally along a tunnel when dirt supports them, or dropped by digging underneath — falling apples crush creeps and the player; creeps can push apples too. One bouncing power ball (Space) travels tunnels until it hits a creep or returns; after a kill wait for reform; if stuck, walk over it to retrieve. Creeps later mutate into diggers that carve dirt. Clear by eating all cherries, killing all creeps, or spelling EXTRA (Alphamonster with a letter at score milestones — ball-kill lights that letter; full EXTRA = extra life and advance). Creep touch or apple crush loses a life. Mr. Do needs idle, walk/dig (per direction), and throw frames; creeps need walk and digger-mutate frames. Controls: Arrow keys move/dig, Space throw ball, R restart. HUD: score, lives, EXTRA letter strip, scene. Colorful high-resolution top-down sprites matching classic arcade Mr. Do! (green dirt, black tunnels, cherry pairs, yellow apples, red white-faced creeps), transparent backgrounds.
